reason: unvoweled bare stem is genuinely noun (ʿilm) or verb (ʿallama); keep both ranked, never force one
voweling raises the top reading's confidence but the competing reading is still kept; voweling narrows, it does not fake certainty
a multi-letter proclitic (bi + al) over a noun stem; the segmentation candidate is consumed, never rebuilt; segment_candidate_ref indexes a real row
an attached pronoun (hum) is a candidate; a single-letter ending would stay low-confidence (likely a radical), routed to suffix-pronoun-state
a final tanwin-alif is NOT the pronoun na (ends_tanwin_alef); the whole-token reading is kept, no false split
a prefixed preposition must contribute to the hover (with/in peace); the public hover segment must not hide the prefix behind the host noun
a beginner (A1/A2) sees the top reading as a gentle hint with no root/pattern metalanguage; the full competing lattice surfaces only at C1+ via the CEFR gate; the lattice itself is unchanged by level
